title: Audiovisual translation
organisation: The Institute for Translation and Interpreting
further_info: Recommended Reading BRÄUTIGAM, Thomas; PEILER, Nils Daniel (Hrsg.) (2015): Film im Transferprozess: Transdisziplinäre Studien zur Filmsynchronisation. Marburg, Schüren. CHAUME, Frederic (2012): Audiovisual translation: dubbing. Manchester, St. Jerome. _______. (2013): „The turn of audiovisual translation: New audiences and new technologies“, in: Translation Spaces 2, S. 105-123. DÍAZ CINTAS, Jorge; ORERO, Pilar; REMAEL, Aline (2007): Media for all: subtitling for the deaf, audio description, and sign language. Amsterdam, Rodopi. DÍAZ CINTAS, Jorge; ANDERMAN, Gunilla (2009): Audiovisual translation: language transfer on screen. New York, Palgrave Mcmillan. DÍAZ CINTAS, Jorge (2010): „Subtitling“, in: GAMBIER, Yves; VAN DOORSLAER, Luc (Hrsg.), Handbook of Translation Studies. Volume 1. Amsterdam and Philadelphia, John Benjamins, S. 344-349. DÍAZ CINTAS, Jorge; REMAEL, Aline (2014): Audiovisual Translation: Subtitling. 2nd ed. London and New York, Routledge. GAMBIER, Yves; GOTTLIEB, Henrik (2001): (Multi) Media Translation. Amsterdam and Philadelphia, John Benjamins. JÜNGST, Heike Elisabeth (2010): Audiovisuelles Übersetzen. Ein Lehr- und Arbeitsbuch. Tübingen, Narr. MATAMALA, Anna (2019): „Voice-over. Practice, research and future prospects“, in: PÉREZ-GONZÁLEZ, Luis (Hrsg.), The Routledge Handbook of Audiovisual Translation. London, New York, S. 64-81. PANIER, Anne (2012): Filmübersetzung. Probleme bei Synchronisation, Untertitelung, Audiodeskription. Frankfurt am Main, Peter Lang. RITTMAYER, Allison (2009): „Translation and Film: Slang, Dialects, Accents and Multiple Languages“, in: Comparative Humanities Review 3 (1). ZÁRATE, Soledad (2021): Captioning and Subtitling for d/Deaf and Hard of Hearing Audiences. London, UCL Press.
